Title: Up the Glass
Speaker: Director
Setting: Executive elevator, ascending, {corp} logo etched in frosted glass that ripples as the floor numbers climb
TextLines: The summons came on hardline. No subject. Just a floor number — one you've never had access to before.
The elevator passes the executive levels and keeps going. Forty-eight. Forty-nine. The numbers stop being labeled around fifty.
"The board has been watching you," the Director says. {gender:m|He|She} is reading something on a slate, not looking up. "Your last several cycles have been instructive."
Somewhere above you, the building thins to spire. Below, the city is a puddle of light.
"There's an opening at Liaison. Quiet office. Bigger purview. Different… latitude."
The Director finally looks up. "It's not an offer yet. But it's not nothing."

Choices.Label: "Tell me what the board actually wants."
